rulings.Case Title: GRELLAS SHAH LLP VS. RAJ KHAWARE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 7, 2026, Line 1, PETITIONER GRELLAS SHAH LLP's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award. Petitioner Grellas Shah LLP's motion to confirm arbitration award is granted. Good cause shown and no opposition filed. Judgment shall be enter in favor of Grellas Shah in accordance with the final award issued by the Arbitrator in AAA case number 01-24-0007-8561. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK).

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN DAMRON VS. POINTE PACIFIC HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or Alternatively Evidentiary And Or Issue Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 7, 2026, Line 5, DEFENDANT JOHN SIMS' Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or Alternatively Evidentiary And Or Issue Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant John Sims' motion for terminating, evidentiary, and/or issue sanctions is granted in small part. Defendant did not need to meet and confer because this court's order was clear and there was nothing left to resolve. (Leach v. Superior Court (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 902, 906.) Terminating sanctions are unwarranted since the plaintiff has now responded to the discovery and paid the sanctions. (Hultin Decl., pars. 8-10.) "[T]erminating sanctions are to be used sparingly, only when the trial court concludes that lesser sanctions would not bring about the compliance of the offending party." (R.S. Creative, Inc. v. Creative Cotton, Ltd. (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 486, 496.) Plaintiff shall provide the verifications by the time of the hearing. Plaintiff shall pay $1,250 as reasonable sanctions because her failure to comply with this court's previous order lacked substantial justification. Payment to be made within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK).

rulings.Case Title: SONIA FAIZY VS. BA MINUZZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Defendants, File Proofs Of Service Per Ccp And Failure Top Rosecute The Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 7, 2026, Line 6, DEFENDANT BA MINUZZI's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Defendants, File Proofs Of Service Per CCP And Failure To Prosecute The Action. Defendant Ba Minuzzi's motion to dismiss for failure to serve defendants and failure to prosecute the action is denied. The court lacks statutory or rule authority to dismiss a complaint due to asserted delay of less than two years. (Holden v. California Employment Stabilization Commission (1950) 101 Cal. App. 2d 427, 437-438). While the court has inherent authority to dismiss under certain circumstances, it would be an abuse of discretion to do so at this time in this case based on the record before the court. Counsel for the moving party is strongly admonished not to file papers with this or any other court with egregious miscitations of authority such as the demonstrably false quotation on line 1 of page 7 of the memorandum and not to file a declaration largely comprised of argument and inadmissible purported facts. In addition, the motion was not served and filed in compliance with CCP 1005(b) which requires that an additional ten days be added when, as here, the motion papers are served by mail and the place of mailing is outside California. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK).
